ending: Felix and Pit survive the Cardinal confluence by insisting on their separate identities, though Felix gives up most of its authority and keeps a painful wound. A year later, Vessilia is his wife and Empress, ruling an empire that is rebuilding and receiving migrants. Evie represents the King's Rock Coven in Elder Throne. Beef and Hallow continue voluntary spirit-preservation research without a breakthrough. Gabby and the Chanters still cannot open a safe route to Earth, and their latest attempt releases a hostile creature instead. Kevin's invitation to Gabby opens the possibility of a relationship. The Compact remains active, and four Cardinal moons mark the restored powers. The former god of chaos and flame warns Felix that foreign empires are moving toward war, while a thunderbird arrives from the east seeking him.
in_short: Felix leads a rescue into ruined Amaranth, where Vessilia and the other Unbound survive corrupted omen paths and return with greater power. After claiming Amaranth, he prepares for the Ruin while a surviving Hierophant and a dwarven invasion threaten Jast. Felix and Pit recover the Exalted Bell and learn that the gods stole Cardinal power and that the Compact became their flawed prison. At Jast, the allies defeat Volantris and a half-divine, but the Compact breaks and frees the gods. The Whale Maw and a divine corpse help destroy them before the Ruin arrives. Felix learns the Ruin is the transformed god of destruction, becomes scion of the Unseen Tides, restores Pit, kills the god, and reforms the four Cardinal powers. He and Pit reject permanent absorption and return. A year later, Vessilia rules beside Felix, the empire rebuilds, Earth remains unreachable, and foreign war and a thunderbird present new dangers.

recaps.text: Previously: Felix Nevarre became the Nemean Emperor and united a growing alliance while gathering the known Unbound. Pit remained his bonded companion and fellow ruler within their bond. Vessilia Dayne, Felix's partner and a dragon-bonded dragoon commander, entered an omen path in Amaranth and did not return. Gabby, Felix's sister, survived her time as a divine vessel but remained constrained by its consequences. The Hierophant ruled Amaranth as a major divine adversary, while the wider threat called the Ruin approached. Moonfall and the apparent defeat of the Hierophant devastated Amaranth and its realm. Felix's allies included Evie Aren, Beef and Hallow, Archie Ross, Kevin and Shadow, Elowen, Atar V'as, Alistair, Harn, Scylla, and other imperial forces.

Moonfall leaves Amaranth ruined, but the Hierophant survives and drains the weakened moon-associated goddess of her remaining core. In Garion, Felix receives the rewards for his apparent victory and commits to rescuing Vessilia from her omen path. The Unbound and a broad allied force travel aboard a Sunaran ship while Harn remains to guard the empire. Beyond imperial territory, unstable mana creates malformed creatures, realm fissures, and dust-bodied anomalies that reform when struck. Elowen pilots the ship into a hazardous dark passage, where time and distance are uncertain. A Desolation threatens to unmake the vessel, and a colossal whale-mawed entity arrives with corrupted followers. Felix uses fiendstone barriers to keep the ship moving, assigns Beef to reinforce them, and leaps away from the vessel to confront the creature himself.

Felix and Pit fight the Whale Maw long enough for the damaged ship to escape through a rift, then reach the crater where Amaranth once stood. Felix claims the former Hierophant's territory, but instability prevents him from fully uniting it. A moon-shadow echo rises from the crater with an omen door embedded in its chest. When Gabby's attack reveals the door, Felix stops the group from destroying the echo and sends Gabby, Beef, Archie, Evie, Kevin, Shadow, Elowen, and Ondine through a breach. Inside, each person receives a path shaped from past experience. Archie revisits a burglary, Kevin and Shadow return to their teenage lives, and Beef encounters his younger self and learns more about his parents' separation. Beef and Ondine discover that unstable creatures are invading the reconstructed memories, showing that the trials themselves are being corrupted.

The omen paths overlap as corruption spreads. Vessilia and Evie meet in a reconstructed tournament, where Vessilia learns that the Hierophant had her mother framed and executed for protecting border communities. Vessilia leaves with a new vow of retribution. Elowen crosses paths with Kevin and Shadow after unstable mana infects their separate memories. Gabby returns to the night she vanished from Earth and tries to save memory versions of her friend April and her brother Felix. After learning how to make the invading shadow creatures vulnerable, she refuses an offered exit rather than abandon them. Outside, Felix, Pit, Scylla, Wendell, and the army struggle to restrain the growing echo without damaging the door. The echo absorbs its own creatures and becomes stronger, while damage to the moon-shard structure in its chest threatens everyone still inside.

The travelers pass into trials centered on present choices, gaining new skills while the omen structure deteriorates. Gabby chooses Kevin's survival over a fabricated town, and Vessilia, Beef, and others undergo dangerous tempering. When Gabby's former vessel persona urges her to abandon the group, bonds from her companions pull her through the failing threshold. The final path shows a possible future in which unstable mana, falling moons, and the Ruin destroy mortal and divine defenses alike. In that vision, the group learns that unstable creatures can be made solid enough to kill, but the Ruin crushes them. A colossal figure bearing Felix's face then consumes the threat and turns on the world. The vision ends without proving that future inevitable, but it links Felix to a possible world-destroying transformation and charges the group to oppose the coming end.

Gabby rejects the violent identity offered by her path and accepts Felix's aid through their bond. Outside, the moon-shadow remnant absorbs the crater's instability and channels the Ruin against Felix's army. Felix draws the attack onto himself until the omen travelers complete their paths and return. Vessilia and Yin manifest a spectral dragon, Gabby wields an enlarged weapon of light, and the reunited allies destroy the remnant's body. Felix consumes what remains, claims and restores Amaranth, and reaches Grandmaster Tier, though the absorbed instability remains dangerous and the Ruin is still visible. Knowledge, the empire's memory construct, begins searching for the escaped Hierophant. Vessilia's new Path of the First Dragon strengthens her union with Yin, while Felix authorizes empire-wide guidance for willing allies seeking advancement and core manifestations.

Beneath Aeonis, Felix, Vessilia, and Pit find ancestral Gigas spirits who distrust Felix because his authority carries the Destroyer's taint. Gabby's honest account of her coerced transformation persuades the elders to recognize her as Gigas and connect Aeonis to the shadow-gate network without surrendering their independence. The omen travelers compare their new paths. Gabby chooses autonomy over stronger divine options, Beef develops powerful risen constructs, and Knowledge maps possible advancement for Felix's allies. Felix and Vessilia openly affirm their love. Their preparations are interrupted by a foreign incursion: a dwarven empire moves toward the jungle border after elemental rebellions weaken its resources. The supposedly dead Hierophant appears before the invading commander, who invites her across the territorial boundary. Freed from that restraint, she overwhelms his force and takes him captive.

Felix confirms that the Hierophant survived and mobilizes the empire toward Jast, where dwarven forces have crossed the border. Alistair and Atar V'as undertake a mission to seek reinforcements while the army gathers supplies, weapons, and advancement resources. The Cantus offers Felix fealty so his authority can strengthen its incomplete banishment array, though the Song of Exile still cannot reach the Ruin. Vessilia commits herself to confronting the Hierophant. With Felix's authority and the spirit grove, ninety-nine hatchlings emerge as Grove Wardens linked to Felix, Vessilia, Yin, and the grove. They become a new aerial force for the coming war. Archie, now at Master Tier, recognizes troubling changes in his core space but postpones investigating them. Beef and Gabby receive a shared quest that will reward their roles in the campaign.

Felix publicly commits the empire to the Jast campaign, then separates from the main army with Pit to investigate the Sun's End Peaks. Near Korsk, he exposes neglected defenses, opens an ancient Nym ruin to the local Tusk Knights, and dedicates its resources to rebuilding the city. He also receives the Crucible of the Grand Magi, a training artifact that restricts his abilities while rewarding exertion. Felix and Pit learn that an ancient walking city may be the mana source warping the mountain monsters. They begin eliminating the princes and kings controlling the peaks. Even under the Crucible's growing resistance, they kill two mountain kings and consume their domains. The artifact resonates with Felix's regalia and reveals that the domains connect all three realms. One king remains, along with the queen and the mystery beneath the mountains.

Felix and Pit kill the last mountain king and the queen, ending the bloodline that ruled the Sun's End Peaks. The Crucible completes its bodily test and begins a spiritual trial. Beneath the queen's valley, Felix discovers a vast buried corpse whose identity and history remain unresolved. Meanwhile, Vessilia's army reaches a relief city besieged by corrupted dwarves and enslaved elementals. Gabby exposes the hidden elemental assault, but repeated waves breach the city's wards and cause heavy losses. Beef, Hallow, Evie, and the inscriptionists discover that each elemental is controlled by a physical sigil tree. Removing its central glyph can free the host without killing it. The enemy's shadow power immediately destroys liberated elementals, proving the assault is also intended to exhaust the defenders. Vessilia responds by planning a trap of her own.

Inside the mountain-scale corpse, Felix Nevarre and Pit explore an inverted Nym city and its damaged Empyrean Halls. They find libraries of skills and stored core-manifestation memories, then decide their own manifestation should preserve their partnership rather than turn them into a larger predatory being. A trial grants Felix Nevarre an authority key and Pit crescent-bronze claws. Beyond a dead spirit grove, Felix Nevarre reaches the broken Halcyon Throne and finds that a Desolation wound is causing the halls’ dissonance. The Eye of Tumult points through it. Felix Nevarre reconstructs the territorial seal, claims Elysium as inheritor, and restores contact with his allies. He then learns the Ruin will arrive in one day and assigns his memory construct to investigate how the gate might be stabilized or crossed.

Felix uses bonds, ancient oaths, and his new authority to reshape the Desolation wound into a temporary passage. He and Pit cross the Deadlands, where Felix's inner Cardinal beast forces him to absorb part of a buried Cardinal corpse. They continue into a hostile floating-island empire, weakened by its territorial authority. While Vessilia's forces draw the Hierophant toward a civilian refuge, Beef coordinates hundreds of risen in dispersed attacks. The enemy anticipates the evacuations and surrounds the refuge with corrupted dwarves and superior elementals. Felix and Pit infiltrate a palace built on the Obsidian Tumult and activate its ancient authority node. The node offers three questions. Felix begins by asking how the gods became divine and learns that former slaves killed Cardinal primordials, divided their cores, and crowned themselves gods.

The authority node shows Felix how Empyrean exiles joined the Nym and helped build the Compact as a defense against divine rule. A divine attack damaged it during activation, forcing thousands of Empyreans to sacrifice themselves to complete the construct. The Compact became the system and imprisoned the gods, but its original damage left loopholes for vessels and godslaves. Felix's final question asks how to destroy the Ruin, and the answer is that it cannot simply be destroyed. An Empyrean architect intervenes, stresses the importance of bonds and choice, recognizes Felix and Pit's shared path, and gives them the Exalted Bell before the node collapses. On the battlefield, Archie manifests primordial-stone power to save civilians despite severe injury. Gabby fights toward the controlling Imperator as a walking-volcano Supreme Elemental enters the war seeking Felix.

Vessilia and Gabby pursue the sixth Imperator, whose body is controlled by a half-divine of shadow. Elsewhere, Makos dies shielding Kevin from Volantris, after which Kevin and Shadow form their shared manifestation and continue the defense. Atar V'as enters Volantris, destroys the dwarven control machinery, and kills the elemental at a precise weak point. Its collapse kills enemy troops and hundreds of allied soldiers. Vessilia and Gabby kill the Imperator, forcing the half-divine to manifest directly. Vessilia and Yin take true dragon form, and with Gabby and the Grove Wardens they kill the half-divine. Its released core connects to the Ruin and opens a route for greater divine powers. Bound gods begin descending as the empire's restrictions fail. The victory over the immediate invaders has triggered a far more dangerous incursion.

Felix and Pit return from the authority node and kill Emperor Kanad with an incomplete shared core manifestation. They abandon the vacant authority of the floating empire and force an emergency route back to Jast. The god of rot, the bloodstorm goddess, and a pair of divine twins emerge there, raising dwarven troops as godslaves and using stolen cores to weaken their chains. Felix arrives as the Legion struggles to hold the fortifications. Crescent-bronze equipment from the ancient city gives the defenders a brief advantage. Felix consumes the invading army's creatures and constructs, binds the Exalted Bell, and completes the Empyrean Regalia. Its Vital Aegis lets him take allied wounds onto himself within his territory. He injures the bloodstorm goddess by turning her bond-made lightning against her, but the gods use the approaching Ruin to break the last restrictions.

Felix and Pit fight outside a living-tree shield raised around their allies. The god of rot devastates the battlefield, while the twins exploit bonds of fellowship to corrupt chanters and weaken the defenders' banishment array. The Compact shatters, removing the gods' remaining bindings. A modified array called Chains of the Faithful restrains them only briefly. At Felix's direction, a breach brings the Whale Maw from the Deadlands to attack the god of rot. Inside the shield, the Hierophant returns and kills several dragoons. The twins also pull through the mutilated corpse of a divine sibling bearing three cores. The divine prison is broken, the chant is failing, and both the Whale Maw and the sibling corpse have entered the battlefield. Felix and the defenders have only a narrow interval before every restraint collapses.

Gabby kills the Hierophant while Vessilia distracts her, freeing Scylla from a hostage attempt. The divine sibling corpse turns against the twins and kills one, then pours its power and three cores into Felix. Felix's internal Cardinal beast sacrifices its separate existence to stabilize and strengthen Felix's union with Pit. Together they cripple the last twin, and the Whale Maw consumes it. The system confirms the deaths of all four attacking gods. With the divine threat ended, the Ruin descends at once. Felix and Pit enter the Whale Maw, defeat the presence within it, seize its heart, and drive the creature into the Ruin. There they witness the truth: the Ruin is the transformed god of destruction, created through stolen divine power, and the Compact only expelled it beyond the void rather than destroying it.

Inside the Ruin, Felix purifies three altered divine cores and chooses the Unseen Tides. When Ruin appears to erase Pit, Felix refuses to release their bond. The former Cardinal of that domain makes Felix its scion, and Felix uses the Whale Maw's remaining power and his bonds to restore Pit. Pit then loses a wing protecting him as Felix undergoes an irreversible evolution. Felix and Pit strip eight stolen cores from the god directing the Ruin and kill it. A mortal former god gives Felix a core of change, allowing him to reunite four complete Cardinal powers. Felix and Pit begin renewing ruined lands, though they cannot restore the dead, and are drawn into a Cardinal confluence. They preserve their identities and return. One year later, Vessilia rules as Empress and Felix's wife. The empire rebuilds, but Earth remains unreachable, foreign war approaches, and a thunderbird comes seeking Felix.
